exclusions.behaviour: Database-level personal-data redaction after account closure
exclusions.reason: The browser journey proves closure, sign-out, and validation but does not capture the resulting user row.
exclusions.requiredBefore: Any account-closure route is approved for .NET.

exclusions.behaviour: Milestone-event payloads and database ownership by visit versus user
exclusions.reason: The journey proves guest/user branching, persistence through edit, free text, and one-off skipping but does not inspect events or canonical database rows.
exclusions.requiredBefore: Course-feedback write ownership is approved for .NET.

exclusions.behaviour: Ahoy event names, properties, ordering, and completion milestones
exclusions.reason: These internal side effects require a real application side-effect capture scenario rather than UI-only assertions.
exclusions.requiredBefore: Any journey that emits the affected event is approved for .NET.

exclusions.behaviour: Every previous/next/save button variant in the generic feedback renderer
exclusions.reason: Journeys exercise representative guest, registered, update, and persistence paths but not the renderer's complete Contentful question matrix.
exclusions.requiredBefore: The generic feedback renderer boundary is approved for .NET.

exclusions.behaviour: Maintenance-mode routing for health, public, private, and maintenance pages
exclusions.reason: The ordinary journey environment has maintenance disabled and cannot prove both configurations in one application process.
exclusions.requiredBefore: Any application route is switched to .NET in an environment where maintenance mode is required.

exclusions.behaviour: Exhaustive progress-decorator state matrix and every skip-ahead permutation
exclusions.reason: Journeys cover not-started, started, resume, progress, fail, and completion states but not every internal decorator combination.
exclusions.requiredBefore: Module overview/progress ownership is approved for .NET.

exclusions.behaviour: Exact title assertion for every authenticated registration and module-content URL
exclusions.reason: Journeys assert representative titles and page headings; the exhaustive Rails title matrix remains the authoritative control.
exclusions.requiredBefore: Shared layout/title generation is approved for .NET.

exclusions.behaviour: Canonical database rows and milestone-event payloads for pass and fail attempts
exclusions.reason: The browser journey proves learner-visible pass/fail, certificate, and retake behaviour but does not yet capture application writes or events.
exclusions.requiredBefore: Module-completion writes or event production are approved for .NET.
